 {"id":"caam-hc7","title":"Implement 'use' and 'which' commands for default profiles","description":"## Task
Add commands to set and show default profiles per provider.

## `caam use` Command
```bash
caam use codex work-1     # Set work-1 as default for codex
caam use claude personal  # Set personal as default for claude
```

Sets the default profile in config. After this, commands that need a profile but don't specify one use the default.

## `caam which` Command
```bash
caam which           # Show defaults for all providers
caam which codex     # Show default for codex only
```

Output:
```
codex:  work-1
claude: personal
gemini: (none)
```

## Implementation
- Add DefaultProfiles map to config (already exists!)
- use: Update config and save
- which: Read config and display

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can set default profile per provider
- Can view defaults
- Commands that need profile use default when not specified","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:54:29.911282188-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:37:04.378795755-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:37:04.378795755-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 'caam use' and 'caam which' commands for setting and viewing default profiles per provider.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-hc7","depends_on_id":"caam-1ba","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:54:29.932504866-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-hzu","title":"Add AuthModeDeviceCode to provider interface","description":"# Add AuthModeDeviceCode to Provider Interface

## What

Extend the provider.AuthMode enum to include device code flow as a first-class authentication method.

## Why

The OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant (RFC 8628) is fundamentally different from browser-based OAuth:
- No localhost redirect needed
- User authenticates on a separate device
- CLI polls for token completion
- Better UX for headless environments

This deserves its own AuthMode rather than being a flag on OAuth because:
1. Different flow mechanics (polling vs redirect)
2. Different timeout characteristics
3. Different user interaction model
4. Profiles may be configured specifically for device-code-only scenarios

## Implementation Details

### File: internal/provider/provider.go

Add new constant:
\`\`\`go
const (
    AuthModeOAuth      AuthMode = "oauth"       // Browser-based OAuth flow
    AuthModeAPIKey     AuthMode = "api-key"     // API key authentication
    AuthModeDeviceCode AuthMode = "device-code" // OAuth device code flow (RFC 8628)
    AuthModeVertexADC  AuthMode = "vertex-adc"  // Vertex AI ADC
)
\`\`\`

### Interface Extension

Consider adding optional interface for providers that support device code:
\`\`\`go
// DeviceCodeProvider extends Provider with device code flow support
type DeviceCodeProvider interface {
    Provider
    // SupportsDeviceCode returns true if this provider supports device code flow
    SupportsDeviceCode() bool
    // LoginWithDeviceCode initiates device code authentication
    LoginWithDeviceCode(ctx context.Context, p *profile.Profile) error
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AuthModeDeviceCode constant added
- [ ] DeviceCodeProvider interface defined (optional extension)
- [ ] Existing code continues to work (no breaking changes)
- [ ] Documentation comments explain when to use device-code vs oauth

## Testing

- Unit test that AuthModeDeviceCode is distinct from other modes
- Verify SupportedAuthModes() can include multiple modes

## Notes

- Keep backward compatible - existing profiles with "oauth" continue to work
- Profile.AuthMode field accepts the new value
- CLI will need to accept --device-code flag separately","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:36:22.865093603-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:41:58.077570631-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:41:58.077570631-05:00","close_reason":"Added AuthModeDeviceCode constant and DeviceCodeProvider interface to provider package."}
 {"id":"caam-i4g","title":"Prevent path traversal in vault/profile operations","description":"Fresh-eyes review: user-supplied tool/profile args flow into filepath.Join and then os.RemoveAll in internal/authfile.Vault.Delete and internal/profile.Store.Delete. If a user passes an absolute path (e.g. profile-name '/' or tool '/') filepath.Join can escape the intended base directory and RemoveAll can delete arbitrary paths. Fix by validating tool/profile segments (no separators, no abs/volume, not '.'/'..') inside the authfile/profile packages before any filesystem mutation, and add unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:42:04.88163533-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:50:04.329067996-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T21:50:04.329067996-05:00","close_reason":"Validated tool/profile segments in authfile vault + isolated profile store to prevent filepath.Join escape and unsafe os.RemoveAll; added unit tests.","comments":[{"id":35,"issue_id":"caam-i4g","author":"ubuntu","text":"Investigating now. Plan: add strict segment validation + safe join in internal/authfile and internal/profile before any file ops (especially Delete/Restore/Create), add unit tests for rejecting '/', '..', and separators. Will run go test + ubs, then bd sync + push.","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:42:24Z"}]}
-{"id":"caam-i5i","title":"Add text input dialog component to TUI","description":"# Task: Add dialog components to TUI

## Context
The TUI needs reusable dialog components for multiple features: backup (text input), export/import (confirmation), and sync machine management (multi-field input).

## Implementation

### Location
`internal/tui/dialog.go` (new file)

### Components Needed

#### 1. TextInputDialog - Single field input
- Title/prompt text
- Text input field (use bubbles/textinput)
- Submit action (Enter)
- Cancel action (Esc)
- Validation callback
- Placeholder text

#### 2. ConfirmDialog - Yes/No confirmation
- Title/prompt text
- Configurable button labels (Yes/No, OK/Cancel, etc.)
- Default selection
- Submit returns bool

#### 3. MultiFieldDialog - Multiple input fields
For "add machine" and similar:
```
┌─ Add Machine ─────────────────────────┐
│ Name:     [_______________]           │
│ Address:  [_______________]           │
│ SSH Key:  [_______________]           │
│                                       │
│        [Cancel]  [Add]                │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```
- Array of field definitions (label, placeholder, validator)
- Tab/Shift+Tab between fields
- Submit when all valid

### Bubble Tea Pattern
```go
type DialogType int

const (
    DialogTextInput DialogType = iota
    DialogConfirm
    DialogMultiField
)

type DialogModel struct {
    dialogType  DialogType
    title       string
    active      bool
    
    // For text input
    input       textinput.Model
    validator   func(string) error
    errorMsg    string
    
    // For confirm
    confirmLabel string
    cancelLabel  string
    confirmed    bool
    
    // For multi-field
    fields      []FieldModel
    focusIndex  int
    
    // Callbacks
    onSubmit    func(result interface{}) tea.Cmd
    onCancel    func() tea.Cmd
}

type FieldModel struct {
    Label       string
    Input       textinput.Model
    Validator   func(string) error
    ErrorMsg    string
}
```

### Styling
- Use existing lipgloss styles for consistency
- Center dialog on screen
- Semi-transparent overlay behind dialog
- Clear visual focus on active input field
- Error messages in red below field

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TextInputDialog component works (single field)
- [ ] ConfirmDialog component works (yes/no)
- [ ] MultiFieldDialog component works (multiple fields)
- [ ] Tab navigation between fields in multi-field dialog
- [ ] Proper key handling (Esc cancels, Enter submits)
- [ ] Validation support with error display per field
- [ ] Integrates cleanly with existing TUI model
- [ ] Follows existing code style and patterns

## Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `internal/tui/dialog.go`
- Create: `internal/tui/dialog_test.go`
- Modify: `internal/tui/model.go` (add dialog state)
- Modify: `internal/tui/update.go` (handle dialog keys)
- Modify: `internal/tui/view.go` (render dialog overlay)

## Why Multi-Field?
The sync "add machine" feature needs 3 fields (name, address, SSH key path). Without multi-field support, we'd need 3 sequential dialogs which is poor UX.

## Dependencies
Part of epic: caam-bvd (TUI Backup Feature)
Used by: TUI Backup, TUI Export/Import, TUI Sync Management","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:39:16.223031181-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:03:30.52879596-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:03:30.52879596-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: Created internal/tui/dialog.go with TextInputDialog, ConfirmDialog, and MultiFieldDialog components. Includes validation support, Tab/Shift+Tab navigation, and comprehensive tests in dialog_test.go. All 17 dialog tests pass."}

 {"id":"caam-i5j","title":"Signal handler for graceful operations","description":"## Purpose
Implement signal handling for SIGHUP-triggered reload and graceful shutdown.

## Background
Unix signals provide a standard way to communicate with running processes.
SIGHUP traditionally means "reload configuration".
SIGINT/SIGTERM mean "shutdown gracefully".

## Signals to Handle

| Signal | Action | Use Case |
|--------|--------|----------|
| SIGHUP | Reload profiles | External script triggered refresh |
| SIGINT | Graceful shutdown | Ctrl+C in terminal |
| SIGTERM | Graceful shutdown | Process manager (systemd, docker) |
| SIGUSR1 | Dump stats to log | Debugging/monitoring |

## Technical Implementation

### Signal Handler Package
Create internal/signals/signals.go with Handler struct that listens for
signals and exposes channels for reload, shutdown, and debug events.

### TUI Integration
Add watchSignals() tea.Cmd that listens to the signal handler channels
and emits appropriate messages (reloadRequestedMsg, tea.Quit, dumpStatsMsg).

### CLI for Sending Signals
Add `caam reload` command that finds TUI PID and sends SIGHUP.

### PID File Management
Write PID file on TUI start, remove on clean shutdown.

## Files to Create
- internal/signals/signals.go
- internal/signals/signals_test.go
- cmd/caam/reload.go

## Platform Considerations
- SIGHUP/SIGUSR1 not available on Windows
- Use build tags for platform-specific implementations
- Windows alternative: named pipe or file-based signaling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SIGHUP triggers profile reload in TUI
- [ ] SIGINT/SIGTERM cause graceful shutdown
- [ ] SIGUSR1 dumps stats to log file
- [ ] PID file created/removed correctly
- [ ] caam reload command works
- [ ] Graceful degradation on Windows","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:25:08.969830867-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:09:59.597288125-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T21:09:59.597288125-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented signal handling + pid file + caam reload: internal/signals handler, TUI integration for SIGHUP reload / SIGINT+SIGTERM quit / SIGUSR1 stats log, and reload CLI command. Tests added.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-i5j","depends_on_id":"caam-0gx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:30.518098355-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":32,"issue_id":"caam-i5j","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting: implement internal/signals handler (SIGHUP reload, SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown, SIGUSR1 dump), integrate into TUI (reload + quit), manage PID file, and add caam reload command. Agent mail not configured; coordinating via bd comments.","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:00:33Z"},{"id":33,"issue_id":"caam-i5j","author":"ubuntu","text":"Landed in commit 7e59594: added internal/signals (Handler + pid/log helpers), TUI now writes ~/.caam/caam.pid (CAAM_HOME-aware), handles SIGHUP reload, SIGUSR1 dump-to-log, SIGINT/SIGTERM graceful quit, and added caam reload command to send SIGHUP using the pid file. Added unit tests for pid + signals + reload command registration.","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:10:04Z"}]}

 {"id":"caam-nac","title":"Implement browser launcher abstraction","description":"## Task
Create internal/browser/browser.go with a Launcher interface and implementations for different browsers.

## Design
```go
type Launcher interface {
    // Open opens a URL in the configured browser profile
    Open(url string) error
    // Name returns the browser name for display
    Name() string
}

type Config struct {
    Command    string // Browser executable
    ProfileDir string // Profile directory or name
}
```

## Implementations Needed
1. ChromeLauncher - uses --profile-directory flag
2. FirefoxLauncher - uses -P flag for profile name
3. DefaultLauncher - uses OS default (open/xdg-open)

## Platform Considerations
- macOS: Chrome is at /Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome
- Linux: Usually 'google-chrome' or 'chromium-browser'
- Windows: Complex paths, need registry lookup or common paths

## Rationale
Abstraction allows us to support multiple browsers and add new ones easily. The interface also makes testing straightforward.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Launcher interface defined
- Chrome implementation works on macOS and Linux
- Firefox implementation works
- Default fallback works
- Unit tests for each implementation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:26.47883372-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:16:04.783685539-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:16:04.783685539-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented browser launcher abstraction with Launcher interface, ChromeLauncher (--profile-directory), FirefoxLauncher (-P), DefaultLauncher (xdg-open/open), and GenericLauncher. Includes platform-specific browser detection for macOS/Linux/Windows.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-nac","depends_on_id":"caam-4eg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:26.499689437-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-nh8","title":"Protected profile naming conventions","description":"Implement special handling for system-managed profiles (prefix with underscore).

## Behavior
- Profiles starting with '_' are 'system profiles' (e.g., _original_claude, _backup_20251217)
- System profiles are created automatically by safety features
- Deleting a system profile requires --force flag
- List command shows system profiles with [system] marker or dimmed

## Profile Types
- `_original_<tool>`: Pre-caam state, created on first activate
- `_backup_<timestamp>`: Auto-backups from smart backup feature

## Implementation
1. Add IsSystemProfile(name) helper in internal/authfile
2. Update Vault.Delete() to check for system profile + require force
3. Update list command to visually distinguish system profiles
4. Add metadata field to meta.json: {type: 'system'|'user', created_by: 'auto'|'user'}

## Files
- internal/authfile/authfile.go: Add helpers
- cmd/caam/cmd/root.go: Update delete command
- cmd/caam/cmd/root.go: Update list command","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:44:01.173048591-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T22:48:12.574768038-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T22:48:12.574768038-05:00","close_reason":"Added system profile convention (_ prefix): ls marks [system], vault delete refuses system profiles unless forced, and backup meta.json now includes type/created_by.","comments":[{"id":50,"issue_id":"caam-nh8","author":"ubuntu","text":"[ubuntu at 2025-12-18]
Claimed. Implementing system profile conventions (`_`-prefixed): add helper(s) in internal/authfile, prevent delete without --force, and visually mark system profiles in `caam ls`/`caam status` outputs where relevant. Will keep it backward compatible with existing vault meta.json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:41:37Z"}]}

-{"id":"caam-npy","title":"Implement token freshness comparison and sync algorithm","description":"# Task: Implement token freshness comparison and sync algorithm

## Context
The core logic that determines which token is "fresher" and should be synchronized. This is the brain of the sync system.

## Token Freshness

### What Makes a Token Fresh?
OAuth tokens contain expiry information. A "fresher" token:
1. Has a later expiry time (primary criterion)
2. Was created/modified more recently (tiebreaker)

### Token Structure Analysis

#### Claude Token (`.claude.json`)
```json
{
  "oauthToken": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "expiry": "2025-12-20T14:29:00Z"
  }
}
```
Key field: `oauthToken.expiry`

#### Codex Token (`auth.json`)
```json
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": 1734623340
}
```
Key field: `expires_at` (Unix timestamp)

#### Gemini Token (`settings.json`)
```json
{
  "oauth_credentials": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "expiry": "2025-12-20T14:29:00Z"
  }
}
```
Key field: `oauth_credentials.expiry`

### Freshness Extractor
```go
package sync

// TokenFreshness represents the freshness of a token
type TokenFreshness struct {
    Provider    string
    Profile     string
    ExpiresAt   time.Time
    ModifiedAt  time.Time
    IsExpired   bool
    Source      string // "local" or machine name
}

// ExtractFreshness parses auth files to determine token freshness
func ExtractFreshness(provider, profile string, authFiles map[string][]byte) (*TokenFreshness, error)

// CompareFreshness returns true if a is fresher than b
func CompareFreshness(a, b *TokenFreshness) bool {
    // Primary: later expiry wins
    if !a.ExpiresAt.Equal(b.ExpiresAt) {
        return a.ExpiresAt.After(b.ExpiresAt)
    }
    // Tiebreaker: later modification wins
    return a.ModifiedAt.After(b.ModifiedAt)
}
```

### Provider-Specific Extractors
```go
type FreshnessExtractor interface {
    Extract(authFiles map[string][]byte) (*TokenFreshness, error)
}

func GetExtractor(provider string) FreshnessExtractor {
    switch provider {
    case "claude":
        return &ClaudeFreshnessExtractor{}
    case "codex":
        return &CodexFreshnessExtractor{}
    case "gemini":
        return &GeminiFreshnessExtractor{}
    }
}
```

## Sync Algorithm

### Sync Operation
```go
type SyncOperation struct {
    Provider    string
    Profile     string
    Direction   string    // "push" or "pull"
    Machine     *Machine
    LocalFresh  *TokenFreshness
    RemoteFresh *TokenFreshness
}

type SyncResult struct {
    Operation   *SyncOperation
    Success     bool
    BytesSent   int64
    BytesRecv   int64
    Duration    time.Duration
    Error       error
}
```

### Full Sync Algorithm
```go
func (s *Syncer) SyncWithMachine(m *Machine) ([]*SyncResult, error) {
    results := []*SyncResult{}
    
    // 1. Connect to remote
    client, err := s.pool.Get(m)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("connection failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer s.pool.Release(m.ID)
    
    // 2. Get local profiles
    localProfiles := s.vault.ListAllProfiles()
    
    // 3. Get remote profiles
    remoteProfiles, err := s.listRemoteProfiles(client)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("list remote profiles: %w", err)
    }
    
    // 4. Merge profile lists (union)
    allProfiles := mergeProfileLists(localProfiles, remoteProfiles)
    
    // 5. For each profile, compare and sync
    for _, p := range allProfiles {
        op := s.determineSyncOperation(client, p)
        if op == nil {
            continue // Already in sync
        }
        
        result := s.executeOperation(client, op)
        results = append(results, result)
    }
    
    return results, nil
}
```

### Determine Sync Operation
```go
func (s *Syncer) determineSyncOperation(client *SSHClient, p ProfileRef) *SyncOperation {
    localFresh := s.getLocalFreshness(p)
    remoteFresh := s.getRemoteFreshness(client, p)
    
    switch {
    case localFresh == nil && remoteFresh != nil:
        // Only exists on remote: pull
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "pull", ...}
        
    case localFresh != nil && remoteFresh == nil:
        // Only exists locally: push
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "push", ...}
        
    case CompareFreshness(localFresh, remoteFresh):
        // Local is fresher: push
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "push", ...}
        
    case CompareFreshness(remoteFresh, localFresh):
        // Remote is fresher: pull
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "pull", ...}
        
    default:
        // Equal freshness: no action
        return nil
    }
}
```

### Execute Sync Operation
```go
func (s *Syncer) executeOperation(client *SSHClient, op *SyncOperation) *SyncResult {
    start := time.Now()
    
    switch op.Direction {
    case "push":
        // Read local files
        files, err := s.vault.ReadProfileFiles(op.Provider, op.Profile)
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
        // Write to remote
        remotePath := s.remoteProfilePath(client.machine, op.Provider, op.Profile)
        err = client.BatchWrite(remotePathFiles(remotePath, files))
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
    case "pull":
        // Read remote files
        remotePath := s.remoteProfilePath(client.machine, op.Provider, op.Profile)
        files, err := client.BatchRead(remoteFilePaths(remotePath))
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
        // Write to local vault
        err = s.vault.WriteProfileFiles(op.Provider, op.Profile, files)
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
    }
    
    return &SyncResult{
        Success:  true,
        Duration: time.Since(start),
        ...
    }
}
```

## Conflict Resolution

### Simultaneous Updates
If two machines update the same profile at nearly the same time:
- Both will detect their local as "fresher"
- Both will push
- Result: last write wins (deterministic by timestamp)

This is acceptable for v1. Future enhancement: conflict detection and resolution UI.

### Expired Tokens
Even expired tokens might be useful (for refresh_token). Don't skip expired tokens in sync.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Freshness extraction for Claude tokens
- [ ] Freshness extraction for Codex tokens
- [ ] Freshness extraction for Gemini tokens
- [ ] Freshness comparison logic correct
- [ ] Push operation works
- [ ] Pull operation works
- [ ] Union merge of profile lists works
- [ ] Full sync algorithm correct
- [ ] Conflict handling deterministic
- [ ] Unit tests for freshness extraction
- [ ] Unit tests for comparison logic
- [ ] Integration test for full sync

+{"id":"caam-npy","title":"Implement token freshness comparison and sync algorithm","description":"# Task: Implement token freshness comparison and sync algorithm

## Context
The core logic that determines which token is "fresher" and should be synchronized. This is the brain of the sync system.

## Token Freshness

### What Makes a Token Fresh?
OAuth tokens contain expiry information. A "fresher" token:
1. Has a later expiry time (primary criterion)
2. Was created/modified more recently (tiebreaker)

### Token Structure Analysis

#### Claude Token (`.claude.json`)
```json
{
  "oauthToken": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "expiry": "2025-12-20T14:29:00Z"
  }
}
```
Key field: `oauthToken.expiry`

#### Codex Token (`auth.json`)
```json
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": 1734623340
}
```
Key field: `expires_at` (Unix timestamp)

#### Gemini Token (`settings.json`)
```json
{
  "oauth_credentials": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "expiry": "2025-12-20T14:29:00Z"
  }
}
```
Key field: `oauth_credentials.expiry`

### Freshness Extractor
```go
package sync

// TokenFreshness represents the freshness of a token
type TokenFreshness struct {
    Provider    string
    Profile     string
    ExpiresAt   time.Time
    ModifiedAt  time.Time
    IsExpired   bool
    Source      string // "local" or machine name
}

// ExtractFreshness parses auth files to determine token freshness
func ExtractFreshness(provider, profile string, authFiles map[string][]byte) (*TokenFreshness, error)

// CompareFreshness returns true if a is fresher than b
func CompareFreshness(a, b *TokenFreshness) bool {
    // Primary: later expiry wins
    if !a.ExpiresAt.Equal(b.ExpiresAt) {
        return a.ExpiresAt.After(b.ExpiresAt)
    }
    // Tiebreaker: later modification wins
    return a.ModifiedAt.After(b.ModifiedAt)
}
```

### Provider-Specific Extractors
```go
type FreshnessExtractor interface {
    Extract(authFiles map[string][]byte) (*TokenFreshness, error)
}

func GetExtractor(provider string) FreshnessExtractor {
    switch provider {
    case "claude":
        return &ClaudeFreshnessExtractor{}
    case "codex":
        return &CodexFreshnessExtractor{}
    case "gemini":
        return &GeminiFreshnessExtractor{}
    }
}
```

## Sync Algorithm

### Sync Operation
```go
type SyncOperation struct {
    Provider    string
    Profile     string
    Direction   string    // "push" or "pull"
    Machine     *Machine
    LocalFresh  *TokenFreshness
    RemoteFresh *TokenFreshness
}

type SyncResult struct {
    Operation   *SyncOperation
    Success     bool
    BytesSent   int64
    BytesRecv   int64
    Duration    time.Duration
    Error       error
}
```

### Full Sync Algorithm
```go
func (s *Syncer) SyncWithMachine(m *Machine) ([]*SyncResult, error) {
    results := []*SyncResult{}
    
    // 1. Connect to remote
    client, err := s.pool.Get(m)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("connection failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer s.pool.Release(m.ID)
    
    // 2. Get local profiles
    localProfiles := s.vault.ListAllProfiles()
    
    // 3. Get remote profiles
    remoteProfiles, err := s.listRemoteProfiles(client)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("list remote profiles: %w", err)
    }
    
    // 4. Merge profile lists (union)
    allProfiles := mergeProfileLists(localProfiles, remoteProfiles)
    
    // 5. For each profile, compare and sync
    for _, p := range allProfiles {
        op := s.determineSyncOperation(client, p)
        if op == nil {
            continue // Already in sync
        }
        
        result := s.executeOperation(client, op)
        results = append(results, result)
    }
    
    return results, nil
}
```

### Determine Sync Operation
```go
func (s *Syncer) determineSyncOperation(client *SSHClient, p ProfileRef) *SyncOperation {
    localFresh := s.getLocalFreshness(p)
    remoteFresh := s.getRemoteFreshness(client, p)
    
    switch {
    case localFresh == nil && remoteFresh != nil:
        // Only exists on remote: pull
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "pull", ...}
        
    case localFresh != nil && remoteFresh == nil:
        // Only exists locally: push
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "push", ...}
        
    case CompareFreshness(localFresh, remoteFresh):
        // Local is fresher: push
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "push", ...}
        
    case CompareFreshness(remoteFresh, localFresh):
        // Remote is fresher: pull
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "pull", ...}
        
    default:
        // Equal freshness: no action
        return nil
    }
}
```

### Execute Sync Operation
```go
func (s *Syncer) executeOperation(client *SSHClient, op *SyncOperation) *SyncResult {
    start := time.Now()
    
    switch op.Direction {
    case "push":
        // Read local files
        files, err := s.vault.ReadProfileFiles(op.Provider, op.Profile)
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
        // Write to remote
        remotePath := s.remoteProfilePath(client.machine, op.Provider, op.Profile)
        err = client.BatchWrite(remotePathFiles(remotePath, files))
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
    case "pull":
        // Read remote files
        remotePath := s.remoteProfilePath(client.machine, op.Provider, op.Profile)
        files, err := client.BatchRead(remoteFilePaths(remotePath))
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
        // Write to local vault
        err = s.vault.WriteProfileFiles(op.Provider, op.Profile, files)
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
    }
    
    return &SyncResult{
        Success:  true,
        Duration: time.Since(start),
        ...
    }
}
```

## Conflict Resolution

### Simultaneous Updates
If two machines update the same profile at nearly the same time:
- Both will detect their local as "fresher"
- Both will push
- Result: last write wins (deterministic by timestamp)

This is acceptable for v1. Future enhancement: conflict detection and resolution UI.

### Expired Tokens
Even expired tokens might be useful (for refresh_token). Don't skip expired tokens in sync.

 {"id":"caam-pb7","title":"Enhance lock file with PID validation","description":"## Task
Improve the lock file system to detect stale locks from dead processes.

## Current Lock Format
```json
{"pid": 12345, "locked_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"}
```

## Enhanced Implementation
1. Read lock file
2. Check if PID still exists (os.FindProcess + signal 0)
3. If process dead, consider lock stale
4. Optionally auto-clean stale locks

## Platform Considerations
- Unix: kill(pid, 0) returns error if process doesn't exist
- Windows: OpenProcess + different error handling

## Code Location
- internal/profile/profile.go - IsLocked(), Lock(), Unlock()
- Add: IsLockStale(), CleanStaleLock()

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can detect if locking process is still running
- Stale locks reported appropriately
- Works on Linux and macOS","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:08.139140246-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:24:04.458474061-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:24:04.458474061-05:00","close_reason":"Added LockInfo struct, GetLockInfo(), IsProcessAlive(), IsLockStale(), CleanStaleLock(), and LockWithCleanup() methods. The exec runner now uses LockWithCleanup to automatically clean stale locks from dead processes.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-pb7","depends_on_id":"caam-pxg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:29.6771176-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-peb","title":"Usage analytics TUI panel","description":"## Purpose
Add a usage statistics panel to the TUI showing profile usage patterns visually.

## Background
The TUI provides a richer interface than CLI. Usage stats can be displayed in a
dedicated panel or overlay, accessible via keystroke.

## Design Mockup
```
┌─ Usage Statistics ─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│  Last 7 days                                               │
│                                                             │
│  claude/work         ████████████████████  42 sess  18.5h  │
│  codex/main          ████████████████      28 sess  12.1h  │
│  claude/personal     ██████                12 sess   4.2h  │
│  gemini/default      █                      3 sess   0.5h  │
│                                                             │
│  Total: 85 sessions, 35.3 hours                            │
│                                                             │
│  Press [u] to toggle, [1-4] for time range                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Keyboard Shortcuts
- `u` - Toggle usage panel visibility
- `1` - Last 24 hours
- `2` - Last 7 days (default)
- `3` - Last 30 days
- `4` - All time
- `esc` - Close panel

## Technical Implementation
```go
// internal/tui/usage_panel.go
type UsagePanel struct {
    visible   bool
    timeRange int // 1=24h, 7=week, 30=month, 0=all
    stats     []ProfileUsage
    width     int
    height    int
    styles    UsagePanelStyles
}

type ProfileUsage struct {
    Provider     string
    ProfileName  string
    SessionCount int
    TotalHours   float64
    Percentage   float64 // For bar chart
}

func (u *UsagePanel) SetStats(stats []ProfileUsage)
func (u *UsagePanel) Toggle()
func (u *UsagePanel) SetTimeRange(days int)
func (u *UsagePanel) View() string

// Bar chart rendering
func renderBar(percentage float64, width int) string {
    filled := int(percentage * float64(width))
    return strings.Repeat("█", filled) + strings.Repeat(" ", width-filled)
}
```

### Integration with Main Model
```go
// internal/tui/model.go
type Model struct {
    // ... existing fields
    usagePanel *UsagePanel
}

func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
    switch msg := msg.(type) {
    case tea.KeyMsg:
        if key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Usage) {
            m.usagePanel.Toggle()
            if m.usagePanel.visible {
                return m, m.loadUsageStats()
            }
        }
    }
}
```

- internal/tui/usage_panel_test.go
- internal/tui/model.go (add usagePanel field)
- internal/tui/keys.go (add Usage keybinding)

- [ ] Bar chart shows relative usage
- [ ] Time range switching works
- [ ] Stats load asynchronously (no UI freeze)
- [ ] Graceful handling when no data available
- [ ] Panel respects terminal size","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:23:16.833524781-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:20:24.669416806-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:20:24.669416806-05:00","close_reason":"Added TUI usage stats panel with toggle, time ranges, async loading, and tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-peb","depends_on_id":"caam-r36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:02.150717061-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-peb","depends_on_id":"caam-cfb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:07.256718993-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"caam-peb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting TUI usage panel: add internal/tui/usage_panel.go + keybindings (u/1-4/esc), async stats load from internal/db activity_log, and tests for rendering + ranges.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:13:14Z"},{"id":25,"issue_id":"caam-peb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented Usage Statistics overlay in TUI: new UsagePanel with bar chart + totals, 'u' toggles, '1-4' time ranges, 'esc' closes, and async stats loading from SQLite activity_log. Added unit tests for panel rendering and model key handling.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:20:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-pxg","title":"Session Management","description":"## Overview
Enhance the existing lock file system to provide robust session tracking and prevent data corruption when multiple processes try to use the same profile.

## Current State
- Basic lock files exist (profile.Lock(), profile.Unlock())
- Lock files contain simple JSON with PID and timestamp

## Problems to Solve
1. Stale locks: If a process crashes, the lock remains
2. No visibility: Can't see what sessions are running
3. No protection: Operations don't always check locks

## Goals
- Detect and clean up stale locks (process no longer running)
- Show running sessions in CLI and TUI
- Prevent destructive operations on locked profiles
- Allow force-unlock for stuck locks

## User Value
- Prevents accidental data corruption
- Makes it safe to run multiple caam instances
- Visibility into what's running","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:03.044026907-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:42:02.293200799-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:42:02.293200799-05:00","close_reason":"All child tasks completed: sessions list, lock file PID validation, and force-unlock commands."}
